definition:
ὁρίζω
 (ὅρος, a boundary), [in LXX for אָסַר, גְּבוּל, etc. ;] 
  1. to separate, mark off by boundaries (so Num.34:6, Jhn.13:27). 
   2. to determine, appoint, designate: of time, with accusative, Act.17:26, Heb.4:7; with accusative of person(s), Act.17:31; with inf., Act.11:29; pass., Luk.22:22, Act.2:23 10:42, Rom.1:4 (cf. ὀφ-, ἀπο- δι-, τρο-ορίζω),†
 (AS)
